revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{ { {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name={{ill|Iwatsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsuta Shrine|qq=}} | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}} {{ill|Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku|qid=Q296095|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku}} Hamadera Ishizuchōnaka 4-chōme 12-7 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|24|N|135|27|12|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine)
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | 創建=Traditionally attributed to the 7th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} (469 BCE) | 本殿=Single-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival=Saturday near {{ill|October 5|en|October 5|qid=Q2930|lt=October 5|1=October 5}} | 神事={{ill|Tōka-Ebisu|ja|十日戎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōka-Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす講|1=Ebisu-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11261037}}, {{ill|Hatsu-uma|ja|初午|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu-uma|1=Hatsuuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q510847}}, Hachiman Festival, Yassai Hossai | image=File:Iwatsuta-jinja haiden.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Nishi-ku|qid=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.55667|N|135.45333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.556667|135.453333}}|caption=Iwatsuta Shrine|width=256}} '''Iwatsuta Shrine''' (石津太神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku|qid=Q296095|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, it is one of the candidate locations for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Iwatsuta Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} alongside {{ill|Ishizu Shrine|ja|石津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizu Shrine|1=Ishizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11586788}} in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|qid=Q1042008|lt=Sakai-ku, Sakai|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}. Both claim to be the "oldest Ebisu shrine in Japan." == Enshrined Deities == The principal deities enshrined are {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|八重事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940685}} (ancestral deity of the Ishizutsure clan). {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (from Tsunagami Shrine) is also worshiped, along with {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} through shrine amalgamation. == History == According to legend, this area was once a seaside location where {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} set {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} adrift, eventually reaching this site. It is said that Hiruko placed five sacred stones ("Goshiki-no-Ishi") here, naming the place "Ishizu." The location where these stones were interred is marked near the intersection before the shrine's torii gate. The shrine was built on August 10 in the 7th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, per imperial command. Over the years, it received patronage from the Imperial Court, including visits by emperors. Although the shrine possessed extensive lands, it suffered destruction during the {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|qid=Q635245|lt=Genna era|1=Genna}} and later conflicts. During the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q321242}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} revered the shrine as a guardian deity. {{ill|Kimura Shigenari|en|Kimura Shigenari|qid=Q840196|lt=Kimura Shigenari|1=Kimura Shigenari}} contributed gold to reconstruct the shrine. In the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era|1=Meiwa}}, it resolved a dispute over {{ill|Ofuda|en|Ofuda|qid=Q2500908|lt=ofuda|1=Ofuda}} distribution with {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|qid=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, gaining a broader following. == Shrine Buildings == The main shrine consists of two halls: the northern hall, housing Hiruko, Kotoshironushi, and Ame-no-Hohi, is built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style, while the southern hall, dedicated to Takeminakata and Amaterasu, is in {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style. Both halls are connected by a shared gabled worship hall constructed during the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki era|1=Hōreki}}. The grounds include a large stone where Hiruko is said to have rested and several auxiliary shrines such as {{ill|Iwatsuta Inari Shrine|ja|磐山稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsuta Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579301|1=Iwatsuta Inari Shrine}}, Hachiman Shrine, and White Snake Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine is known for its fire festival, the "Yassai Hossai," held on {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|qid=Q2382|lt=December 14|1=December 14}}. Participants write wishes on paper attached to 108 sacred wooden bundles, which are burned to divine fortunes based on the falling direction of the flames. Men carrying an effigy of {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} perform fire-walking while chanting "Yassai Hossai." This ritual is believed to offer protection from pain and misfortune.Goto, Michi. "Japanese Strange Festivals," Seikyusha, 1996. ISBN 4787231308. == See Also == * {{ill|Futon Daiko|ja|ふとん太鼓|1=Futon Daiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futon Daiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Futon Daiko|qq=}} * Izumiotsu Educational Center - The shrine's deity Kotoshironushi was enshrined here as "Ebisu Daimyōjin."“Izumiotsu and Its First Danjiri Parade,” Sakai Senshū No. 10. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Iwatsuta-jinja}} * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=32 Sakai Tourism Guide: Iwatsuta Shrine] {{translated page|ja|石津太神社|version=83482350|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Iwatsuta-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Iwatsuta Shrine (Q11586779)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Iwatsuta Shrine|lt=Iwatsuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|commons|Category:Iwatsuta-jinja|qid=Q11586779|1=Iwatsuta Shrine}} || — || — || part of {{ill|Tonoki Shrine|qid=Q55532347|1=Tonoki Shrine}} || {{ill|Ihatsutano- Shrine|lt=Ihatsutano- Shrine|qid=Q135039162|1=Ihatsutano- Shrine}} || {{coord|34.556670|135.453330|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180976 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11586779}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q11586779]] [[ja:石津太神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
